summary:
The arterial supply to the submandibular gland originates primarily from the facial artery, a branch of the external carotid artery. This makes Option D—the facial artery—correct based on established anatomical references.  

Note: Ensure no mention of "agent" or "reasoning".

The submandibular gland is supplied by the facial artery, a terminal branch of the external carotid artery. This establishes the facial artery as the correct answer due to consistent anatomical documentation.
